name: Cundi Buddha Mother Practice
description: Purification and wish-fulfillment practice through Cundi Buddha Mother (准提佛母), the Great Mother of seven million Buddhas. One of the most widely practiced esoteric Buddhist sadhanas in East Asian Buddhism, uniquely accessible because it does NOT require formal empowerment (abhisheka).
deity: Cundi Buddha Mother (准提佛母 / Cundī Bhagavatī)
mantra_short: 嗡 折隸 主隸 準提 娑婆訶
mantra_complete_sanskrit: Namaḥ Saptānāṃ Samyaksaṃbuddha Koṭīnāṃ Tadyathā Oṃ Cale Cule Cuṇḍe Svāhā
mantra_complete_chinese: 南無 颯哆喃 三藐三菩提 俱胝喃 怛姪他 嗡 折隸 主隸 準提 娑婆訶
empowerment_note: A unique property of Cundi practice: it can be practiced by ANYONE without formal abhisheka. Oral transmission (lung) from a qualified teacher is traditionally beneficial but not strictly required.
visualizations: 18-armed Cundi Buddha Mother seated on a white lotus, white/golden-yellow body, three eyes, radiating compassionate dignity
White or yellow light radiating from her body and from each of her 18 hands, entering the practitioner and purifying all karmic obscurations
Each of her 18 arms offering a different Dharma blessing (vajra, wheel, lotus, sword, etc.), each removing a specific class of obstacle
Nectar-like light flowing from her heart center, dissolving all negativity into golden radiance at the solar plexus
Fire element: the purifying flame of Cundi's wisdom consuming karmic obscurations without harming the practitioner
benefits: Protection from obstacles to spiritual practice
Can be practiced without empowerment (empowerment-free path)
Spiritual accomplishment (siddhi) of purification, magnetizing, and pacifying
Complements the 88 Buddhas repentance practice as a purification pair
frequency_meaning: Solfeggio 417 Hz — facilitating change and undoing situations; resonates with Cundi's power to dismantle karmic obstacles
color_note: WHITE or YELLOW (golden) — varies by tradition; white for purity, yellow/gold for the fire element and solar plexus correspondence
mudra: Cundi Mudra (准提印) — fingers interlocked with both index fingers pointing upward, forming a pointed peak

iconography.arms_meaning: The 18 distinctive qualities of a Buddha (āveṇika-dharmāḥ)

practice_structure.refuge_and_bodhicitta: Generate refuge and bodhicitta motivation before beginning recitation
practice_structure.mantra_recitation: Recite the complete dharani or the heart mantra 'Oṃ Cale Cule Cuṇḍe Svāhā' on a 108-bead mala
practice_structure.target_accumulation: 100,000 repetitions as traditional foundation; may be continued indefinitely
practice_structure.dedication: Dedicate all merit for the awakening of all sentient beings

complementary_practices: 88 Buddhas Great Repentance (八十八佛大忏悔文) — pairs powerfully as a combined purification practice
Great Compassion Mantra (大悲咒) — complementary Guanyin/Avalokiteshvara practice
